note: Baseline watering intervals assume average indoor conditions; the agent adjusts them using live weather, placement (indoor/outdoor), and days since last watered. Values are guidance, not guarantees. weather_tolerance.max_category is the harshest daily weather category (0 sunny, 1 cloudy, 2 rainy, 3 thunderstorm, 4 snow) the plant safely tolerates outdoors for a day; min_safe_temp_c is the lowest safe overnight/day-low temperature. light_tier.min/max is the range of light intensity (0 low/shade, 1 medium indirect, 2 bright indirect, 3 direct sun) the plant thrives in, curated from light.need.

plants.moisture_check: Push a finger ~5 cm (2 in) into the soil — only water if it is bone dry at that depth. When in doubt, wait.

placement.notes: Best kept indoors in cool, wet climates like Dublin.

plants.moisture_check: Water when the top 3-5 cm of soil feels dry to the touch.

placement.notes: Tender; keep indoors in Ireland.

plants.moisture_check: Water when the top feels dry or the leaves start to droop slightly — it perks back up soon after watering.

placement.notes: Keep indoors; dislikes cold.

plants.moisture_check: Water when the top 3-5 cm of soil is dry.

placement.notes: Indoor in temperate climates.

plants.moisture_check: Water when the top 2-3 cm of soil is dry; tolerates occasional missed waterings.

placement.notes: Indoor; can summer outdoors in shade.

plants.moisture_check: Let the soil dry out completely; check 5 cm down and only water if fully dry. Overwatering is the main killer.

placement.notes: Indoors in Dublin; can go out in warm, dry spells.

plants.moisture_check: Water only when the soil is dry most of the way down; when unsure, wait a few more days.

placement.notes: Indoor; very forgiving.

plants.moisture_check: Check daily in warm weather; water when the top 1-2 cm is dry. Wilting leaves mean it is thirsty.

placement.notes: Sunny windowsill indoors, or outdoors in warm spells.

plants.moisture_check: Water deeply when the top 3-5 cm of soil is dry; roses like a thorough soak, not constant damp.

placement.notes: Happiest outdoors in full sun; hardy through Irish winters.

plants.moisture_check: Water when the top 3-5 cm of soil is dry; it dislikes both drought and soggy roots.

placement.notes: Indoor; sensitive to cold draughts.

plants.moisture_check: Keep evenly moist — water when the surface just begins to dry; never let it dry out fully.

placement.notes: Indoor; loves humidity.

plants.moisture_check: Water when the top 2-3 cm is dry; keep evenly moist but not soggy.

placement.notes: Hardy outdoors in Ireland; also grown indoors.

plants.moisture_check: Let the soil dry between waterings; err on the dry side. Soggy soil causes root rot.

placement.notes: Needs full sun and sharp drainage — challenging in cloudy Dublin; a sheltered, sunny south-facing spot helps.

plants.moisture_check: Water deeply and consistently; check daily in warm weather. Irregular watering causes fruit splitting.

placement.notes: Outdoors or greenhouse in summer; needs warmth and sun.

plants.moisture_check: Let the soil dry fully; check 5 cm down and only water if dry. Wrinkled leaves signal it needs a drink.

placement.notes: Indoor in Ireland; can go out in warm, dry spells.

plants.moisture_check: Water when the top 3-5 cm of soil feels dry.

placement.notes: Tender tropical; keep indoors in temperate climates.

placement.notes: Indoor; dislikes cold draughts.

plants.moisture_check: Water when the top 3-5 cm of soil is dry; tolerates occasional dryness.

placement.notes: Indoor; keep away from cold.

plants.moisture_check: Water when the top 3-5 cm is dry.

placement.notes: Indoor; keep warm.

plants.moisture_check: Keep evenly moist and never let it dry out fully; it prefers filtered or rain water.

placement.notes: Indoor; loves humidity and hates cold, dry air.

plants.moisture_check: Keep the soil lightly moist; water when the surface starts to dry.

placement.notes: Indoor; humidity-loving.

plants.moisture_check: Water about weekly by soaking the bark and draining fully; never leave it sitting in water.
